meta.description: Act 1 Climax — The Registration Crisis. Faction conflicts converge, the riot erupts, early endings occur, and survivors transition to Act 2.

climax_001.title: Emergency Broadcast
text: The screens change all at once. Every holographic billboard, every public terminal, every cracked datapad in every pocket across every district. The same face appears: Director Harlan Voss of Meridian Stack Services, immaculate in a charcoal suit, his smile engineered to convey warmth while delivering a knife.

"Citizens of the Greater Metropolitan Zone," Voss begins, his voice smooth as polished chrome. "In light of recent incidents involving unregulated consciousness transfer technology and the growing threat of black-market cortical stacks, the Meridian Board of Directors, in partnership with the Municipal Authority, has authorized Emergency Directive Seven-Seven-One."

He pauses. The pause is rehearsed. "Effective immediately, all residents are required to report to their nearest Meridian Stack Registration Center for mandatory cortical mapping and stack registration. This includes citizens who do not currently possess a stack. Compliance is required within seventy-two hours. Non-compliance will result in restricted access to municipal services, employment, and housing."
The broadcast ends. The street goes quiet for exactly three seconds. Then the noise begins.

text.text: Your wireless receiver catches the unencrypted metadata riding beneath the broadcast signal. Hidden in the data is a secondary directive, flagged for law enforcement only: non-compliant citizens are to be detained. Synths without current registration are to be deactivated and disassembled for parts.

The void ripples around you as the broadcast plays. Cortical mapping would expose what you are. The instruments they use to read human brains would find something else entirely in yours. Something that would put you in a lab instead of a database.

choices.text: Listen to the crowd's reaction
Check your datapad for more details on the directive

climax_002.title: The Street Reacts
text: The Lower District erupts. Not into violence, not yet, but into the raw sound of people realizing the walls just closed in tighter. Voices layer on top of each other, a chorus of fear and fury.

A woman clutching two children screams at the nearest public terminal as if Director Voss can hear her. An old man with a prosthetic jaw sits down on the curb and puts his head in his hands. A group of orc dockworkers are already pulling the metal shutters down on a nearby warehouse, fortifying.
"Mandatory registration," someone near you spits. "They want to map every brain in the city. Tag us like livestock. You know what happens after they map you? They own the copy. They own YOU."

Across the street, a Meridian Sec-Corp drone adjusts its patrol pattern, sweeping its scanner beam across the growing crowd with mechanical indifference. It's already counting faces. Already filing reports.

text.text: The dockworkers see you and one of them waves you over, urgent. "They'll come for us first," she says, her tusks bared. "They always do. The Gene Riots started with 'mandatory testing' too. We need to move. Now."

Being baseline suddenly feels like a curse with a new edge. You have no augments to flag, no non-human biology to expose. You could register and disappear into the system. But the system is the cage, and once they map your brain, they have a copy of everything you are.

choices.text: Head to Mako's shop for information
Check your datapad for faction messages
Follow the orc dockworkers to the warehouse

climax_003.title: The Datapad Floods
text: Your datapad is vibrating nonstop. Messages flood in, layered on top of each other, some encrypted, some broadcast openly in defiance of every surveillance protocol.
From the Iron Collective, unencrypted and furious: ALL COMRADES REPORT TO THE FOUNDRY. THIS IS THE LINE. WE DO NOT REGISTER. WE DO NOT COMPLY. COMMANDER VEX SPEAKS AT 2100 HOURS.

From the Neon Court, encrypted behind three layers of social cipher: Darlings, the Queen requests your presence at the Prism Lounge for an 'impromptu celebration.' Dress code: fabulous. Subtext: mandatory. The Court sees opportunity in chaos.

From the Circuit Saints, broadcast on a narrow-band frequency: The Observatory is entering lockdown. All acolytes return immediately. The registration system will attempt to interface with the city network. We must protect the data divine. High Compiler Ezra-7 has spoken.
From the Ghost Syndicate, a message that deletes itself as you read: Chaos is profit. Stations assigned. Check dead drops. The Broker says: harvest everything.

From the Ashen Circle, carved into the static between channels like a whisper from the void itself: The mapping will fail. Consciousness cannot be contained. Come to the Ossuary. We will show them what lies beneath the mind.

text.text: A private message arrives from an unknown sender: "The black-market stacks that started this crisis? Meridian planted them. The whole thing is manufactured. The directive was written months ago. They were waiting for an excuse. You found the crack in the wall. Now they're sealing it. — A Friend"

choices.text: Head to Mako's shop to figure out your next move
Go directly to your faction's rally point
You're unaligned. Find somewhere safe to think.

climax_004.title: Mako's Warning
text: Mako's shop is in controlled chaos. She's pulled the metal shutters halfway down and is soldering something to the underside of her workbench with frantic precision. Two kids from the street crew are stuffing data chips into a bag. The air smells of burnt circuits and fear.
"Took you long enough," Mako says without looking up. "Sit down. Don't touch anything."

She finishes the solder joint and straightens up. Her augmented eyes are cycling through spectra you can't see, scanning the street through the shutters. "Registration isn't about safety. It's about control. Meridian has been lobbying for mandatory cortical mapping for years. The black-market stack crisis gave them the excuse they needed."

She pulls a battered map of the city onto the workbench and stabs her finger at the Transit Hub. "The Iron Collective is organizing a mass protest here. Thousands of workers, maybe more. Commander Vex is calling it the 'Last Stand.' If Meridian tries to enforce registration by force, this is where it starts."

Her finger moves to the Spire district. "The Neon Court is hosting what they're calling a party. It's a war council. They're deciding whether to back Meridian or undercut them. The Court goes where the power is."
She looks at you directly. "Everyone is choosing sides. You need to choose yours. Tonight."

choices.text: "What are YOU doing, Mako?"
"Where's Kira? Is she safe?"
"I've already chosen. I'm heading to my faction."
"I'm not picking a side. I'm surviving this alone."

climax_005.title: The Dockworkers' Forge
text: The warehouse is already half-fortress by the time you arrive. Orc dockworkers and human laborers work side by side, welding scrap metal over windows, stacking crates into barricades, distributing improvised weapons from a crate that definitely wasn't here yesterday.
A massive orc woman with a shaved head and hydraulic arms directs the operation like a general. You recognize the Iron Collective sigil welded onto her shoulder plate. She sees you and nods once.

"Registration centers open in twelve hours," she says. "Meridian Sec-Corp is already deploying enforcement drones to every district. They're not waiting for the deadline. Anyone who looks like they might resist is getting flagged for preemptive detention."

She hands you a wrench the size of your forearm. "Commander Vex is rallying everyone at the Transit Hub tonight. Biggest protest this city has seen since the Gene Riots. You want to stand with us, you show up. You want to hide, nobody here will judge you. But the clock is ticking and there's no neutral ground left."

text.text: She grips your shoulder. Her hydraulic hand could crush steel but the touch is gentle. "Our people were registered once before. Cataloged. Tagged. You know what came after. We will not line up again."

choices.text: "I'll be at the Transit Hub. Count on it."
Help fortify the warehouse before heading out
"I need to check on some people first."

climax_006.title: Alone in the Storm
text: You find a quiet corner behind a decommissioned water reclamation unit, the kind of place where the drone patrol routes leave a gap. The city hums around you, vibrating with tension you can feel in your teeth.

No faction. No allies. Just you and whatever you've managed to scrape together since waking up in that rented coffin three months ago. The street preacher's words echo in your memory: in a city that wants to own your mind, the only free people are the ones nobody is looking for.
But Meridian is about to start looking for everyone. Mandatory registration means mandatory visibility. No more gaps in the system. No more shadows to hide in.

From here you can see three different routes. The Transit Hub, where the Iron Collective's protest is massing. The Neon Strip, where the Court's 'celebration' glitters like a trap made of diamonds. And the tunnels, leading down into the sewer network and the Undercity beyond, where the truly desperate go to disappear.

text.text: The void hums at the edge of your perception. Down in the Undercity, the Ashen Circle is preparing something. You can feel it, a resonance in the dimensional frequencies that only your kind can sense. They're calling. Not to you specifically. To everything that exists between the cracks.

Your HUD runs a tactical assessment without being asked. Three scenarios, three probability trees, zero outcomes where you come out untouched. The math doesn't lie: staying neutral is the most dangerous option of all.

choices.text: Head to the Transit Hub — the protest is where things will happen
Go to the Neon Strip — information and opportunity
Descend into the tunnels — disappear entirely
[Coward's path] Flee the city. Leave everything behind.

climax_007.title: Mako's Plan
text: Mako pauses. For a moment the mask slips and you see the exhaustion underneath. The fear she keeps bolted down beneath competence and habit.

"I'm doing what I always do. Surviving." She gestures at the kids packing data chips. "I'm moving everything valuable off-site. The Ghost Network has a dead-storage vault in the Undercity. If Meridian comes through with registration scanners, this shop gets cleaned out. Every client record, every encrypted contact, every piece of data I've brokered in twenty years. Gone."

She picks up a small device from the bench, a localized EMP, homemade. "If they breach the door before I'm done, this fries everything in a six-meter radius. Including my augments. Including me, probably."

She sets the EMP down carefully. "After I'm packed, I'm heading to the Transit Hub. Not for the protest. For the aftermath. When things go wrong, and they will, people will need a mechanic who can patch wounds and fix broken hardware in the field. That's my war."
She looks at you with something approaching warmth. "Your war is your choice. But whatever you decide, decide fast. This city has about twelve hours before it tears itself apart."

choices.text: "Where's Kira?"
Help Mako pack, then head to your faction rally point
Help Mako pack, then head to the Transit Hub

climax_008.title: Kira's Danger
text: Mako's expression tightens. "Kira. Yeah." She pulls up a map on her datapad and points to a blinking marker in the upper slums. "She was at the relief kitchen in Sector 4 when the broadcast hit. I pinged her twenty minutes ago. She's scared but she's smart enough to stay put."

She hesitates. "There's a problem. Kira is a stack heir. Her family has corporate connections to Meridian at the board level. If she registers, they'll flag her identity immediately. Her family has a retrieval order out. She ran from that life, but mandatory registration means they can find her in the system and drag her back."

Mako meets your eyes. "If you care about that girl, you need to get her somewhere off-grid before the registration centers open. The Iron Collective protest might provide enough chaos for cover. Or the Ghost Network might be able to forge her a new identity. But she can't stay in the open. Not anymore."

text.text: The thought of Kira being dragged back to the towers, back to the gilded cage she fought so hard to escape, puts a cold knot in your chest. She trusted you. She trusted this place. And now the walls are closing in on her from every direction.

choices.text: "I'll get Kira to safety first. Then I join the fight."
"The protest is more important. Kira can take care of herself."
"Can the Ghost Network hide her?"

climax_009.title: The March to Transit Hub
text: The streets are full. Not the usual current of desperate anonymity but a river with direction, flowing toward the Transit Hub from every alley and corridor in the Lower District. Workers in factory coveralls. Mechanics with grease-stained hands. Families carrying everything they own. The old. The young. The augmented and the baseline. All moving.

Iron Collective banners appear on the buildings, red cloth stamped with the gear-and-fist emblem, hung from fire escapes and draped across walkways. Someone has spray-painted NO REGISTRATION in letters three meters tall across the side of a water tower.

The crowd grows denser as you approach the Hub. You can hear it before you see it, thousands of voices merged into a sound like a storm gathering strength. Chanting. Singing. The rhythmic clang of tools against metal, the heartbeat of a district refusing to be owned.

And above it all, the drones. Dozens of them, circling in formation, their scanner beams cutting through the evening smog like searchlights over a prison yard. Meridian is watching. Meridian is counting.

text.text: Orc clans have come out in force. You see family groups you recognize from the docks, from the foundries, from the fight pits. They march together, shoulder to shoulder, tusked faces set with the grim determination of a people who know exactly what mandatory registration leads to.

A group of elven refugees falls into step beside you. Their faces carry the particular grief of a people who have lost their homeland before and recognize the signs of it happening again. One of them presses a woven bracelet into your hand. "For luck," she whispers in Old Elvish. "We will need it."

choices.text: Push through the crowd toward the main platform
Stay at the edges — keep your options open

climax_010.title: The Neon Strip
text: The Neon Strip is performing its favorite trick: pretending nothing is wrong. The lights still pulse. The music still throbs from behind frosted glass doors. Beautiful people in beautiful clothes glide between clubs and lounges as if the city below them isn't about to ignite.

But the cracks show if you know where to look. Security teams in Neon Court livery flank every entrance, checking invitations that didn't exist yesterday. The street-level vendors have vanished, their stalls shuttered and empty. And behind the smiles and the champagne, conversations are happening in whispers, in coded gestures, in the language of power recognizing a shift in the wind.

A courier in Court colors intercepts you. "You've been noticed," she says, pressing a holographic invitation into your hand. "The Prism Lounge. Private event. The Queen would appreciate your perspective on current affairs." Her smile is a warning dressed as flattery.

text.text: The invitation is real. Your Court standing grants you access to the inner circle tonight. Whatever Archon Luminara is planning, you'll be in the room when she decides.
An invitation to the inner Court is rare for someone from the Lower District. Either they see value in you, or they see a useful pawn. In the Neon Court, the two are often identical.

choices.text: Accept the invitation. Enter the Prism Lounge.
Decline. Head to the Transit Hub instead.

climax_011.title: The Rally at Transit Hub
text: The Transit Hub is unrecognizable. The vast open platform where cargo trains and passenger transports normally run has been transformed into a sea of bodies, banners, and fury. Thousands of people press together under the vaulted steel ceiling, their breath fogging in the cold air, their voices merging into a wall of sound.

Commander Vex stands on a cargo loader raised above the crowd, her silhouette sharp against the industrial lighting. Red Iron Collective banners frame her like wings. She holds no microphone. She doesn't need one. Her voice, amplified by a jury-rigged speaker system, crashes over the crowd like an iron tide.

"They want to map your mind! They want to copy your consciousness and file it in their servers like inventory! They say it's for safety. They said the same thing before the Gene Riots. Before the Purge Protocols. Before every act of state violence that started with 'for your protection!'"
The crowd roars. The sound shakes dust from the ceiling girders.
"I say NO. The Iron Collective says NO. We will not register. We will not comply. And if they come for us with drones and shock troops, we will show them what the workers of this city are made of!"
The roar becomes a chant. NO REGISTRATION. NO REGISTRATION. It echoes through the Hub and bleeds into the streets beyond, carried by speakers and throats and the sheer weight of collective defiance.

choices.text: Join the chant. Let the crowd feel your presence.
Scan the crowd for familiar faces — allies and threats
Push toward Commander Vex to offer your help
Find the Circuit Saints — Ezra-7 will need help protecting the network

climax_012.title: Reading the Crowd
text: You let your eyes sweep the crowd, reading the patterns. Years in the Lower District have taught you to see the currents beneath the surface.

Near the east entrance, a cluster of Circuit Saints stand apart from the masses, their tech-augmented eyes scanning the network traffic flowing through the Hub's infrastructure. Brother Ohm is among them, his lips moving in what might be prayer or encrypted communication.

At the south loading dock, Ghost Syndicate operatives work the edges of the crowd like pickpockets at a carnival. But they're not stealing wallets. They're planting listening devices, copying datapads, photographing faces. Harvesting information from everyone.

High above on the maintenance catwalks, you spot something that freezes your blood. Figures in grey, Ashen Circle acolytes, positioned at the structural weak points of the Hub ceiling. They're not here to protest. They're here to watch. Or to act, if something goes wrong.
And there, near the main entrance, moving against the flow of the crowd: Kira. Alone. Looking terrified. Looking for someone.

text.text: Your stomach drops. Kira is exposed. In this crowd, with Meridian drones scanning every face, her family's retrieval order will flag her within minutes. She has no idea how much danger she's in.

choices.text: Push through the crowd to reach Kira
Focus on the Ashen Circle operatives above — they're planning something
Report the Ghost Syndicate activity to Commander Vex
Join the Ghost operatives — information is power

climax_013.title: The Prism Lounge
text: The Prism Lounge exists in a different reality from the streets below. Crystal chandeliers refract light into rainbows that slide across walls of living glass. The air tastes of expensive ozone purifiers and synthetic jasmine. A string quartet plays something classical and precisely calibrated to mask conversation.

The Neon Court's elite are gathered in clusters around the lounge, their beautiful faces performing calm while their eyes calculate. Archon Luminara herself occupies a curved divan at the center of the room, draped in a gown that shifts color with her mood. Currently it's a deep, thoughtful violet.
She sees you enter and raises one sculpted finger. The invitation is absolute. You approach.

"The rabble is gathering at the Transit Hub," Luminara says, her voice like honey over a blade. "Commander Vex thinks she can stop Meridian with slogans and rust. How quaint. Meanwhile, Meridian's Director Voss has offered the Court a partnership. Exemption from registration in exchange for our media networks enforcing compliance. He wants us to sell the cage as a feature."
She sips something iridescent. "I haven't decided whether to accept. That depends, in part, on what I hear from the street level tonight. From people like you."

choices.text: "The protest will be massive. Meridian can't contain it without bloodshed."
"Take Meridian's deal. You'll be on the winning side."
[Charisma 12] "The Court should play both sides. Support the protest publicly, deal with Meridian privately."

climax_014.title: The Chant
text: You add your voice to the wall of sound. NO REGISTRATION. NO REGISTRATION. The words vibrate in your chest, in the soles of your feet, in the steel structure of the Hub itself. For a moment you are not one person. You are part of something larger, a single voice in a chorus that refuses to be silenced.

Around you, strangers become allies in the space of a breath. A woman grips your arm and screams the words with tears running down her face. An orc welder pumps his fist in time with the chant. A synth worker stands perfectly still, but the LED strips along their chassis pulse red in synchronization with the crowd's rhythm.
Commander Vex sees the crowd reaching critical mass and raises both fists. The chant shifts, becomes a promise, becomes a weapon: WE ARE THE IRON. WE DO NOT BEND.
And then the drones change formation.

You feel it before you hear it, a subsonic hum that vibrates your teeth and makes your vision blur at the edges. The Meridian enforcement drones overhead have switched from surveillance to tactical configuration. Their crowd-dispersal systems are charging.
Someone screams. The crowd ripples like water disturbed by a stone.

choices.text: Hold your ground. Don't let the fear win.
Look for cover — this is about to get ugly
Use the chaos to complete Luminara's intelligence mission

climax_015.title: Commander Vex's Orders
text: You push through the crowd toward the cargo loader. Iron Collective soldiers recognize you and part to let you through. Commander Vex looks down from her makeshift stage, her augmented eyes locking onto you with tactical precision.

"You. Good timing." She drops to one knee at the edge of the platform, bringing her face close to yours. Up close, the scars on her neck tell stories of factory fires and corporate negligence. Her voice drops below the crowd noise.

"Meridian has three registration centers in the Lower District. If those centers go live tomorrow, it's over. People will register out of fear. Once they're mapped, they're controlled." She pulls a data chip from her vest. "I need someone to lead a team to the Sector 7 center and shut it down. Not destroy it. Capture it. The data inside proves Meridian has been illegally mapping citizens for months. We blow this open and the whole directive collapses."
She presses the chip into your hand. "Team of six. Rust is your second. You breach the center during the protest when Meridian's forces are stretched thin. Get the data. Get out. Can you do this?"

text.text: This is it. The mission that proves your worth to the Collective. Commander Vex doesn't hand out leadership roles to people she doesn't trust. Whatever you've done to earn this, it's about to be tested.

choices.text: "I'll do it. Give me the details."
"I can do this, but I want to save someone first. There's a woman named Kira in the crowd."
[Wisdom 12] "Commander, the Ashen Circle has operatives on the catwalks above. Something else is happening."

climax_016.title: Reaching Kira
text: You fight through the crowd, elbowing past bodies packed tight as cargo. The chanting makes the air shake. Above, the drones circle tighter.

Kira sees you and the relief on her face is immediate and total. She grabs your arm with both hands, her knuckles white. "I didn't know where else to go," she says, her voice barely audible over the crowd. "My apartment building is crawling with Meridian officers. They're doing pre-registration sweeps. Door to door."

She's shaking. "If they scan me, they'll find my family's stack registration. My old identity. They'll flag me and my father's retrieval team will have me within the hour." Her eyes are wide, the privileged girl who ran to the slums colliding with the reality that the slums can't protect her anymore.
"I need to disappear," she says. "Really disappear. But I'm not leaving without making sure you're okay. I'm not leaving without—" She stops. The drones overhead change pitch. The crowd surges.
"What's happening?" Kira whispers.

choices.text: "Stay close to me. I'll protect you."
"You need to leave. Now. Before the drones lock the exits."
[Charisma 13] "Kira, I need you to be brave. Hide in the maintenance tunnels under the Hub. I'll come for you when this is over."

climax_017.title: The Ashen Circle Watches
text: You turn your attention upward. The maintenance catwalks run along the ceiling of the Transit Hub like a steel spiderweb, and the grey-clad figures of the Ashen Circle move along them with unsettling purpose.

They're not just watching. They're placing devices at structural junction points. Small, dark objects pressed into the shadows where the catwalks meet the support columns. From the ground, nobody would notice. But you do.

High Priestess Sable herself stands at the central catwalk junction, her grey robes making her nearly invisible against the steel. She looks down at the crowd with an expression of serene detachment, the way a surgeon looks at a patient on the table.

You count seven devices. Seven structural points. If those are charges, they could bring the ceiling down on thousands of people. Or they could be something else entirely. With the Ashen Circle, the line between salvation and annihilation is drawn in fog.

text.text: Your void-sense reads the devices. They're not explosives. They're dimensional anchors, void-tech designed to thin the barrier between realities. The Ashen Circle isn't planning to destroy the Hub. They're planning to open it to the void. To show everyone what lies beneath the skin of the world.

The void resonates with the devices. You can feel them humming in a frequency that makes your cells vibrate. Dimensional anchors. If activated, they would peel back reality itself. The Ashen Circle is preparing a mass revelation. The crowd below has no idea what they're about to witness.

choices.text: Try to reach the catwalks and confront the Ashen Circle
Warn Commander Vex about what you've seen
Do nothing. Let the Ashen Circle play their hand.

climax_018.title: Luminara Considers the Streets
text: Luminara's gown shifts to amber as she considers your words. Around her, Court advisors exchange glances weighted with calculation.

"Bloodshed," she repeats, tasting the word. "How distasteful. And how predictable. Vex has been waiting for an excuse to play revolutionary. Meridian has been waiting for an excuse to play tyrant. They deserve each other."

She sets down her glass. "The Court's interest is not in ideology. It is in position. If the protest succeeds, we want to be seen as sympathetic. If it fails, we want to be seen as uninvolved. If it turns violent, we want to profit from the reconstruction."

She rises. The room adjusts around her like a solar system rearranging itself. "Go to the Transit Hub. Be my eyes and ears. Report what you see. If the situation can be leveraged, I want to know the moment it happens."
She presses a comm device into your hand, gold-plated and feather-light. "This line connects directly to me. Use it wisely."

choices.text: Accept the mission. Head to the Transit Hub as the Court's agent.
Take the comm device but plan to use it for your own purposes

climax_019.title: Backing Meridian
text: Luminara's gown turns a cold, calculating silver. Several Court members lean forward, interested. One of them, a man with chrome cheekbones and eyes like polished obsidian, nods approvingly.
"Pragmatic," Luminara says. "I appreciate pragmatism. Director Voss would certainly be grateful for Court support. And grateful corporate directors make generous partners."

She stands and circles you slowly, her gaze appraising. "But you understand what you're advocating. The protest will be crushed. Workers will die or be forcibly registered. The Lower District will be locked down and mapped, mind by mind. And anyone associated with the resistance will be archived."

Her voice drops to a whisper. "Archived. Such a clean word for such a filthy process. Their bodies recycled. Their consciousness stored in Meridian servers. Technically alive. Practically imprisoned. Forever."
She stops in front of you. "Is that the side you want to be on? Because once the Court commits, there is no changing course. The Neon Court does not hesitate. And it does not forgive betrayal."

choices.text: "I'm sure. Back Meridian. This is the smart play."
"Maybe I was wrong. What other options do we have?"

climax_020.title: The Double Game
text: Luminara's gown cycles through three colors in rapid succession before settling on a deep, pleased gold. The Court members around her go still. Even the string quartet seems to hold its breath.

"Now that," Luminara says softly, "is thinking like a courtier." She rises from the divan and takes your arm, guiding you to the window. Below, the city stretches out in layers of light and shadow. You can see the glow of the protest gathering at the Transit Hub.

"Support the protest publicly. A Court statement condemning mandatory registration. Media coverage sympathetic to the workers. It costs us nothing and buys us the love of the lower city." Her eyes gleam. "Meanwhile, private channels to Meridian remain open. When the crisis passes, we negotiate from a position of moral authority AND corporate access. Brilliant."

She squeezes your arm. "You have a future in the Court, if you survive tonight. Go to the Transit Hub. Make sure the right people see a Neon Court representative standing with the workers. And keep this." She presses the gold comm device into your hand. "I want to hear everything."

choices.text: Head to the Transit Hub as the Court's public face

climax_021.title: Into the Tunnels
text: The tunnels swallow you. The noise of the city above fades to a distant rumble, replaced by the echo of dripping water and the hum of ancient infrastructure. Down here, the Lower District's sewer network connects to maintenance corridors that predate the current city by decades, maybe centuries.

The walls are slick with bioluminescent moss that casts everything in a sickly green glow. Cables thick as your arm run along the ceiling, some still carrying power to systems nobody remembers building. The air is heavy and warm, tasting of recycled water and rust.

You're not alone down here. Shadows move in the peripheral corridors. The Undercity has its own population, people who fell through even the Lower District's threadbare safety net. They watch you pass with hollow eyes.

Ahead, the tunnel branches. One path leads deeper, toward the Ossuary and the Ashen Circle's territory. Another curves upward, connecting to the Transit Hub's sub-level infrastructure. A third descends into darkness so complete your eyes can't parse it.

text.text: The void is stronger here. The dimensional barriers are thin in places, worn by whatever the Ashen Circle has been doing in the Ossuary. You can feel reality flexing like a membrane, and on the other side, something vast and patient is aware of your presence.

choices.text: Follow the path to the Transit Hub sub-level
Head toward the Ossuary — find the Ashen Circle
Descend into the deepest tunnels. Disappear completely.

climax_022.title: Iron Mission: Briefing
text: Commander Vex pulls you behind the cargo loader where a small group waits. You recognize Rust, the old cyborg scavenger, checking a battered pulse pistol with the practiced ease of someone who has done this too many times. Beside him, four Iron Collective soldiers, two humans and two orcs, armed with a mix of industrial tools and repurposed weapons.

"Your team," Vex says. "Rust knows the route. The Sector 7 registration center is in the old municipal building on Forge Street. Meridian has converted it overnight. Scanners, processing terminals, holding cells for non-compliant citizens. Light security right now because they've pulled most of their forces here to watch the protest."

Rust spits on the floor. "Light security means four guards, two automated turrets, and a combat synth on standby. Plus whatever networked defenses they've got running on the inside. Light for Meridian. Fatal for most."

Vex hands you a datachip. "Building schematics. Rust's team breaches the side entrance. You lead. Get to the central server, download the pre-registration data that proves they've been illegally mapping citizens, and get out. Twenty minutes. After that, the protest either holds or breaks, and either way, Meridian's attention comes back to the centers."
She grips your shoulder. "Don't die in there. We need that data more than we need martyrs."

choices.text: "Let's move. Every minute counts."
[Tech 11] "Let me study the schematics first. I want to find a way past those turrets."

climax_023.title: Iron Mission: The Registration Center
text: Forge Street is deserted. Everyone who can walk has gone to the protest. The registration center squats at the end of the block like a bunker disguised as a public building, its windows replaced with armored panels, Meridian's corporate logo glowing above the entrance in sterile white.
Rust signals from the alley mouth. Two fingers. Then a fist. Two guards visible. Hold position.

You press against the wall and watch. The guards are Meridian Sec-Corp, full tactical gear, pulse rifles, the works. But they're bored. Their attention is on the distant sound of the protest, not on the empty street behind them. Behind the guards, you can see the faint blue shimmer of an active scanner grid covering the main entrance.
Rust materializes beside you. "Side entrance is through the loading dock. One camera, one turret. I can loop the camera feed for thirty seconds. The turret is the problem."

text.text: The schematics show a maintenance access panel near the turret's power supply. If someone can reach it without being detected, they can cut the feed. It's a six-meter dash across open ground.

choices.text: [Dexterity 14] Sprint to the maintenance panel and cut the turret's power
[Tech 12] Hack the turret remotely using the building schematics
[Strength 13] Rip the turret off its mount before it can fire

climax_023a.title: The Turret Opens Fire
text: The turret's targeting laser finds you a half-second before its barrel rotates. The first burst tears into the concrete where you were standing a heartbeat ago. Rust grabs your collar and hauls you behind a support column as pulse rounds chew the wall to fragments.
"So much for quiet!" Rust shouts over the gunfire. The turret sweeps the loading dock in methodical arcs. The rest of the team flattens against the walls, pinned.
But the noise accomplishes something stealth couldn't. The two front guards rush around the corner to investigate, leaving the main entrance unguarded for precious seconds. Rust sees it too.
"Go!" he yells. "I'll handle the turret! Get inside and get that data!"

choices.text: Rush the main entrance while the guards are distracted

climax_024.title: Inside the Registration Center
text: The interior of the center is clinical and cold. White walls, white floors, white light. Processing booths line both sides of a central corridor, each one equipped with a cortical scanner, a chair with restraint straps, and a terminal. Some of the booths still have half-eaten meals and personal items on the counters. The staff evacuated in a hurry when the protest started.

The central server room is at the end of the corridor, behind a reinforced door with a biometric lock. Through the glass panel you can see the blinking towers of Meridian data processing equipment, row after row of servers humming with stolen consciousness.

A combat synth stands motionless in the corridor between you and the server room. Seven feet of military-grade synthetic muscle and armor plating, its eyes dark, currently in standby mode. If it activates, this mission gets exponentially harder.

Rust appears behind you, breathing hard. "Turret's down. Guards are zip-tied in the loading dock. We have maybe fifteen minutes before Meridian dispatch sends a response team. That door needs a code or a bypass."

choices.text: [Tech 12] Hack the biometric lock from the nearest terminal
[Strength 13] Force the reinforced door open
Try to sneak past the combat synth while it's in standby

climax_024a.title: The Data Download
text: You're in. The server room hums with the sound of a hundred thousand stolen thoughts. Data streams across every screen, cortical maps rendered as glowing neural webs, each one a person's entire mind reduced to terabytes.
Rust plugs Commander Vex's datachip into the primary terminal. The download begins. Progress bar: one percent. Two percent. The data is massive.

"They've been mapping people for months," Rust whispers, scrolling through the files. "Pre-registration. Citizens who went to Meridian clinics for basic health scans, for augmentation maintenance, for flu shots. Every one of them got cortically mapped without consent. Thousands of people."

He looks at you. The old scavenger's face is grey with fury. "This isn't registration. This is a draft. They're building an army of backed-up minds they can deploy into synthetic bodies. Workforce. Military. Whatever they need. Slave labor that doesn't know it's enslaved because the copies think they're still free."
The download hits sixty percent. An alarm screams through the building. The combat synth in the corridor activates.

choices.text: Press on.
Assess the situation.

climax_024b.title: The Synth Wakes
text: Your attempt triggers the security system. The combat synth's eyes blaze to life, two pinpoints of red in the clinical white corridor. It unfolds from its standby position with mechanical precision, seven feet of death rising to full height.
"UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS DETECTED," it announces in a voice devoid of everything human. "LETHAL FORCE AUTHORIZED."
Rust swears and brings up his pulse pistol. "Fight or run, kid. Pick one now!"

choices.text: Try another approach.
Alternate route.

climax_024c.title: Data Secured
text: The combat synth crashes to the floor, sparks erupting from the ruin of its chassis. The guards are down. The alarm is still screaming but the download bar reads: one hundred percent. Complete.

Rust yanks the datachip free and holds it up like a trophy. His hands are shaking. Blood runs from a cut above his eye. "Got it. Every last byte. Enough evidence to bury Meridian's registration program and half their board of directors."
He pockets the chip and grabs your arm. "Time's up. Response team will be here in minutes. We need to get this to Commander Vex."

You run. Through the corridor, past the wrecked synth, out the loading dock and into streets that have changed in the twenty minutes you were inside. Smoke is rising from the direction of the Transit Hub. The sound of the protest has shifted from chanting to something rawer. Something worse.
"That's not a protest anymore," Rust says, his augmented eye focusing on the smoke. "That's a riot."

choices.text: Race to the Transit Hub with the data

climax_025.title: Ghost Mission: The Harvest
text: You find the Ghost Syndicate operatives working the edges of the crowd with the efficiency of a surgical team. A woman with mirror-shade implants nods as you approach and presses a small device into your hand, a data siphon, designed to copy the contents of any datapad or comm device within two meters.

"The Broker wants everything," she says, her voice barely a whisper. "Iron Collective communications. Neon Court agent identifiers. Circuit Saint network codes. Meridian drone encryption keys. All of it. The chaos is the opportunity. Everyone is distracted. Everyone is transmitting in the clear because they think the crowd noise gives them cover."

She hands you a list on a burner datapad. Six targets. Six people in this crowd carrying data worth a fortune on the black market. "Get close. Let the siphon do its work. Don't get caught. Don't get involved. The Syndicate profits from the crisis. We don't participate in it."
She melts back into the crowd. You're alone with the siphon and the targets.

choices.text: Work the crowd. Harvest all six targets.
Harvest selectively — skip the Iron Collective targets
Pocket the siphon and join the protest instead. The Syndicate can wait.

climax_026.title: Ghost Mission: Data Harvest
text: You move through the crowd like a ghost. The siphon vibrates gently against your palm each time it locks onto a target and begins its silent extraction. To everyone around you, you're just another body in the press of thousands.
Target one: an Iron Collective lieutenant, his comm device broadcasting rally coordinates in the clear. The siphon copies everything in four seconds.
Target two: a Circuit Saint acolyte with a tablet full of network access codes, her attention fixed on the drone formations above. Three seconds.

Target three: a Meridian undercover agent, his cover so thin you spotted him from across the platform by the way he held his datapad at chest height, recording faces. You harvest his data and he never feels a thing.

The siphon fills with intelligence worth more than most people in this crowd will earn in a lifetime. Corporate secrets. Faction strategies. Surveillance data. In the Ghost Syndicate, information is the only currency that never devalues.

choices.text: [Dexterity 12] Go for the remaining targets before time runs out
That's enough. Get clear before the situation escalates.

climax_027.title: Ghost Mission: Full Harvest
text: The last three targets fall like dominoes. A Neon Court informer with a gold comm device, a transit worker whose clearance codes could open every locked door in the Hub, and a woman who turns out to be Ghost Syndicate herself, a rival operative from a competing cell. That one makes you smile.
Your siphon is full. Six targets, six complete data profiles. The Broker will be pleased. In the Ghost Syndicate, pleasing the Broker is the difference between promotion and disposal.

You pocket the siphon and assess the situation. The crowd's energy has shifted while you worked. The chanting has become sharper, more aggressive. People are pushing against the drone perimeter. Commander Vex's voice has taken on an edge of warning. And the drones, you notice, have dropped altitude.
Something is about to break.

choices.text: Get to the edges of the crowd — you need an exit route
Stay in the center. Whatever happens, you need to see it.
Try to sell the harvested data to multiple factions simultaneously

climax_028.title: The Ashen Circle's Ritual
text: The Ossuary is alive with purpose. Ashen Circle acolytes move through the ruined corridors with candles that burn grey, their flames casting shadows that move independently of the light. The air tastes of ozone and something older, something that makes your teeth ache.

High Priestess Sable stands at the center of a chamber where the walls have been carved with symbols that seem to shift when you're not looking directly at them. She turns as you enter, and her smile is the most terrifying thing you've seen all day.
"You've come. Good. The dissolution accelerates. Meridian thinks they can map consciousness. They think the mind is data. They're about to learn how wrong they are."

She gestures to the devices being prepared by her acolytes, the same dimensional anchors you saw on the catwalks above the Transit Hub. "When we activate these, the barrier between this reality and the void will thin. Not break. Thin. Enough for everyone in the Hub to see what lies beneath. Enough for them to understand that consciousness is not a file to be copied. It is a thread in a fabric that stretches across dimensions."

Her eyes gleam. "Meridian's scanners will overload. Their registration data will corrupt. And every person in that Hub will know, in their bones, that they are more than what can be contained in a stack."

text.text: You can feel the truth in her words. The void is not emptiness. It is fullness, an ocean of consciousness that dwarfs anything Meridian's servers could hold. If Sable opens even a crack, the revelation could change everything.

The dimensional frequencies are already resonating with the anchors. You can feel the void pressing against reality like water behind a dam. Sable is playing with forces she understands better than most but controls less than she believes.

choices.text: "I'll help you. Show me what to do."
"This is insane. You could hurt thousands of people."
"What about the civilians? There are children in that crowd."

climax_029.title: Ashen Mission: Protect the Flock
text: Sable takes your hands. Her fingers are cold. Not cold like poor circulation. Cold like the space between stars.

"Your role is not to open the veil. That is our burden. Your role is to protect the innocent when it opens. The thinning will cause panic. People will see things they are not equipped to understand. Some will run. Some will freeze. Some will break."

She presses a small stone into your palm. It's warm where her hands were cold, pulsing with a steady rhythm like a heartbeat. "This is a void anchor. Personal. It will keep you tethered to this reality when the veil thins. Without it, you would drift. With it, you can move through the thinned space and guide others to safety."
Her expression softens into something almost human. "We are not destroyers. We are liberators. But liberation is terrifying to those who have never known it. Be their shepherd when the wolves come."

choices.text: Take the void anchor and return to the Transit Hub
Refuse the anchor. Step into the void willingly.

climax_030.title: Sable's Conviction
text: Sable's expression doesn't change. The serenity doesn't crack. "The children. Yes. I think of them. I think of them growing up in a world where their minds can be copied and sold. Where their consciousness is corporate property. Where death means nothing because you can be rebooted into a body you didn't choose, to serve purposes you didn't agree to."

She turns to the dimensional anchors. "A moment of fear now. A lifetime of truth after. The veil thinning is not an explosion. It is a revelation. People will see. They will understand. And they will never allow Meridian to reduce their minds to data again."

She looks back at you. "You can stand with us or you can return to the surface and face the riot with everyone else. Either way, the veil opens tonight. The only question is whether you will help protect the flock when it does."

choices.text: "Fine. Give me the void anchor. I'll protect the civilians."
Leave the Ossuary. Return to the Transit Hub on your own.

climax_031.title: Saints Mission: Lock Down the Network
text: The Observatory is in full lockdown. Circuit Saints move through the corridors with the focused urgency of monks defending a cathedral. Every terminal is staffed. Every network node is monitored. The air hums with the sound of a thousand processors working at capacity.

High Compiler Ezra-7 stands at the central nexus, his body connected to the Observatory's mainframe by a dozen fiber-optic cables that run directly into the ports along his spine. His eyes are open but seeing something no one else can see, the data architecture of the entire city's network.

"The registration system is already active," he says, his voice carrying the echo of distributed processing. "Meridian has threaded it into the city's core infrastructure. Health records. Transit passes. Employment databases. When they flip the switch, every system in the city becomes a registration scanner. Every interaction a citizen has with any networked device will map their cortical signature."

He turns to you. His eyes focus, partially. "I need you inside the system. Not the physical network. The digital one. I'm going to create a secure tunnel from the Observatory to Meridian's registration backbone. You will navigate the data architecture and plant a corruption seed that will make the registration data unreliable. Garbage in, garbage out."
He holds out a neural interface cable. "This will hurt. And there are defenses inside. Meridian's AI watchdogs are not gentle."

choices.text: Jack in. Fight the system from inside.
[Tech 12] "Let me examine the tunnel architecture first. I want to know what I'm walking into."

climax_032.title: Inside the Network
text: The neural interface connects and reality dissolves. For a terrible, beautiful instant you are everywhere and nowhere, your consciousness stretched across the data backbone of the city like a thought written in lightning.

The cyberspace architecture manifests around you as a vast cathedral of light. Data streams flow like rivers between towering structures of crystalline code. The registration system is visible ahead, a massive black monolith pulsing with stolen neural patterns, each one a citizen's mind reduced to a file.

Meridian's defenses manifest as hunting programs, sleek predatory shapes made of recursive code that patrol the data streams like sharks. They haven't noticed you yet. Ezra's tunnel is masking your signature. But the mask won't hold forever.

You can see the registration backbone's core, a nexus point where all the cortical data converges. Plant the corruption seed there and every registration scan in the city becomes unreliable. Meridian's database turns to noise.

text.text: Cyberspace feels like home in a way the physical world never has. Your synthetic consciousness was designed for digital architecture. Here, you're not a machine pretending to be human. You're a native. And the hunting programs are about to learn what it means to fight something that belongs here.

Your augmented neural pathways give you an edge in cyberspace. The data streams respond to your movements with a fluidity that baseline humans can't match. But the hunting programs are faster than anything you've faced. This will take everything you have.

choices.text: [Tech 13] Navigate the data streams using stealth protocols
[Intelligence 12] Analyze the hunting programs' patrol patterns and find a gap

climax_032a.title: Detected
text: The hunting programs find you. Their code wraps around your consciousness like barbed wire, sending spikes of pain through the neural interface that manifest as white-hot light behind your eyes. In the physical world, your body convulses. In cyberspace, you fight.
Ezra's voice reaches you through the tunnel, distorted by combat interference. "The corruption seed. Plant it. NOW. I'll hold the tunnel open but the defenses are overwhelming my buffers."

You force yourself forward through the attacking code, each step a battle against programs designed to shred unauthorized consciousness. The registration nexus is close. Twenty meters of hostile data architecture between you and the target.

choices.text: Try differently.
Retreat and reassess.

climax_033.title: Corruption Seed Planted
text: You reach the nexus. The registration backbone pulses before you, a tower of stolen minds humming with the concentrated consciousness of thousands. You press the corruption seed into the core and feel it take root, spreading through the data architecture like ivy through brickwork.

The effect is immediate. Registration data begins to scramble. Neural patterns cross-pollinate, merge, dissolve into noise. Every cortical map in Meridian's database is now corrupted beyond recovery. The mandatory registration system is dead before it started.

You pull out. The neural interface disconnects with a sound like tearing cloth and you're back in the Observatory, gasping, your nose bleeding, your hands shaking. Ezra-7's cables retract into his spine as he sags against the mainframe.

"It is done," he says, and for the first time, the High Compiler sounds tired. Tired and triumphant. "The registration system will produce only noise. Meridian's directive is functionally dead. But they don't know that yet. And the riot at the Transit Hub is still happening."

choices.text: Head to the Transit Hub — the situation is still critical
Rest. You've done enough. Let the riot play out.

climax_034.title: Neon Mission: Identify the Leaders
text: You move through the protest crowd with the Neon Court's gold comm device warm in your pocket. Luminara's voice purrs in your ear through the concealed earpiece. "I need names. Faces. The Iron Collective's chain of command. Who are Vex's lieutenants? Who controls the logistics? Who is the weakest link?"

The crowd is a forest of faces. Anger. Fear. Determination. You see Iron Collective soldiers directing traffic, managing supply lines, running communications. Each one is a node in Vex's network. Each one is a name Luminara could use as leverage.

Near the main platform, you spot three key figures: a woman with burn scars coordinating the eastern barricade, a massive orc running the medical station, and a young man with a cybernetic eye managing drone countermeasures.
The gold comm device is ready. One tap and Luminara gets everything.

choices.text: Report everything to Luminara. Complete the mission.
Feed Luminara false information. Protect the Iron Collective.
[Charisma 12] Give Luminara enough real intel to be useful, but hold back the critical details
Smash the comm device. Refuse to spy on desperate people.

climax_035.title: Luminara's Response
text: Luminara's voice returns through the earpiece, layered with the background noise of the Prism Lounge. Crystal glasses. Polite laughter. A universe away from the sweat and fury of the Transit Hub.

"Received. How delightfully organized the working class can be when properly motivated. I'll put this intelligence to good use. The Court's official statement supporting the protest will broadcast in twenty minutes. The Court's private communication to Director Voss will happen simultaneously. Both sides will believe they have our loyalty. Neither will be correct."

She pauses. "Stay in the crowd. The situation is approaching a tipping point and I want eyes on what happens next. If things go very badly, which I suspect they will, find somewhere safe and report the aftermath. The Court profits most from accurate accounting of the damage."

The earpiece clicks off. Around you, the protest crowd shifts. The drones overhead drop lower. Something changes in the air, a tension that goes beyond noise and emotion, like a wire being slowly tightened until it sings.

choices.text: Find cover. This is about to explode.
Push toward the front of the crowd. You need to see what happens.

climax_036.title: The Riot Begins
text: It starts with a single drone. One Meridian Sec-Corp unit descends from the formation and fires a crowd-dispersal taser into the densest part of the protest. The electrical discharge arcs through dozens of bodies in an instant. People scream and collapse. The crowd surges backward like a living thing recoiling from a burn.
Commander Vex's voice cuts through the chaos: "HOLD THE LINE!"

But the line doesn't hold. A second drone fires. Then a third. The organized protest fractures into pockets of fury and panic. Somewhere in the crowd, someone throws a piece of rebar at a drone and connects. The drone spins, sparks, crashes into a support column. The crowd cheers.

Then the combat synths arrive. They emerge from the transit tunnels in precise formation, seven feet of armored plating and military programming, their riot shields locking together into a wall of steel that advances into the crowd at a steady, relentless pace.

The Transit Hub becomes a warzone. Tear gas canisters arc through the air trailing white smoke. The crack of pulse weapons competes with the clang of improvised weapons against synthetic armor. People run. People fight. People fall.

text.text: Orc rage is a real thing. You feel it now, an ancestral fury that rises from somewhere deeper than instinct. Your people were caged before. Cataloged. Hunted. The riot is not a choice. It is a reflex wired into your genetic memory by centuries of oppression.

The combat synths don't register you as a threat. Your synthetic signature reads as non-hostile. But that means you're invisible to their targeting systems, a ghost in the machine. You can move through the chaos where others cannot.

choices.text: Fight the combat synths alongside the Iron Collective
Try to find and protect Mako in the chaos
Try to find and protect Kira in the chaos
Look for an escape route — survival first

climax_037.title: Into the Fray
text: You charge into the line of combat synths alongside a wave of Iron Collective fighters. The noise is deafening. Pulse fire. Screaming. The crash of metal on metal. A synth's riot shield catches you in the chest and you stagger, but someone grabs your arm and pulls you upright.

"Stay with me!" It's Rust, his pulse pistol barking three-round bursts at the synth line. His augmented eye tracks targeting solutions faster than his hand can aim. "Go for the joints! The neck servos! They're armored everywhere else!"

An Iron Collective fighter, a massive orc woman with a sledgehammer, brings it down on a synth's knee joint. The synthetic leg buckles and the machine goes down. The crowd surges over it. But two more step forward to fill the gap.
Above, the drones tighten their formation. A new sound joins the cacophony: the whine of heavy weapons charging. The drones aren't just dispersing anymore. They're escalating.

choices.text: Regroup and push forward.
Fall back.

climax_038.title: Finding Mako
text: You push through the stampeding crowd, fighting against the current of bodies. Tear gas stings your eyes. The noise is a wall of sound that makes thinking nearly impossible.

You find Mako behind an overturned cargo loader, her shop bag clutched to her chest, her augmented arm sparking where a pulse round grazed the casing. She's patching a wounded worker with her good hand, her fingers moving with the calm efficiency of someone who has treated injuries in worse conditions.

"Took you long enough," she says, not looking up from her work. Blood runs down the worker's arm and pools on the platform floor. "Three more wounded behind the loader. I need supplies I don't have and time that doesn't exist."

A drone swoops low and fires. The dispersal taser hits the ground three meters from your position. The electricity arcs through the puddle of blood and Mako throws herself over the wounded worker, taking the residual shock through her augmented arm. She screams but doesn't let go.
"They're shooting at the medics," she gasps. "They're shooting at the goddamn medics."

choices.text: Defend Mako's position — hold the drones off while she works
[Dexterity 12] Grab Mako and the wounded, drag them to better cover
Give Mako your medical supplies and go find Kira

climax_039.title: Finding Kira
text: The crowd is a nightmare of bodies and gas and noise. You push through, scanning faces, screaming Kira's name into a wall of sound that swallows everything.

You find her pinned against a barricade near the east entrance. Two Meridian officers are advancing on her position, their scanners sweeping faces in the crowd. They're not riot control. They're a retrieval team, corporate extraction specialists sent by Kira's family. They've found her.

Kira sees you and her face breaks. She's been crying. One of her hands is bleeding where she caught herself on the barricade. She looks nothing like the sheltered heir who ran to the slums looking for meaning. She looks like a cornered animal.

"They found me," she says. "My father's people. They have my cortical signature. They've been scanning the crowd since the broadcast." She grabs your arm. "Please. If they take me back, I disappear. They'll re-register me, assign me a new stack protocol, and the person I've been for the past year gets archived. Erased. I'd rather die."

choices.text: Fight the retrieval team. Protect Kira at all costs.
[Charisma 13] Bluff the retrieval team — claim Kira is already registered
"Kira, run. I'll hold them off." (Risk: heroic sacrifice)

climax_039a.title: Protecting Kira
text: The retrieval team sees you step between them and Kira. The lead officer, a woman with chrome eyes and the flat expression of corporate enforcement, levels her pulse weapon at your chest.
"Civilian, step aside. This is a private corporate matter. Interfering with a registered retrieval order is a felony."
Behind you, Kira's breathing is ragged. Around you, the riot rages on, providing a curtain of chaos that keeps the rest of the world from noticing this small, desperate standoff.
You don't step aside.

choices.text: Different approach for Kira.
Move forward.

climax_040.title: Finding an Exit
text: Survival instinct takes over. You need out. The Transit Hub has become a kill box, drones above, synths below, tear gas in between. The main exits are choked with fleeing civilians being scanned and sorted by Meridian security. Anyone flagged gets dragged into a holding area. Anyone who resists gets worse.

You spot a maintenance hatch near the southern platform, partially hidden behind a collapsed vendor stall. It leads to the service tunnels beneath the Hub, the same tunnels the transit workers use to access the rail systems. If you can reach it without being spotted, you're out.
The problem is the twenty meters of open ground between you and the hatch. Open ground crawling with drone searchlights and scattered with the fallen.

choices.text: [Dexterity 12] Sprint through the chaos to the maintenance hatch
[Intelligence 11] Wait for a gap in the drone patrol pattern
Forget stealth. Run for it.

climax_041.title: Surviving the Storm
text: You're alive. That word has never meant more than it does right now, pressed against cold metal with smoke in your lungs and blood, yours and others', on your hands.

The worst of the riot is passing. The combat synths have pushed the crowd out of the central platform, but the resistance hasn't broken. Iron Collective fighters hold three barricade positions. Wounded civilians shelter behind overturned cargo loaders. Mako's makeshift medical station is still operating.

From somewhere above, the Ashen Circle's dimensional anchors activate. The air shimmers. For a terrible, beautiful moment, the fabric of reality becomes translucent and everyone in the Hub, everyone, sees what lies beneath. A vast, dark ocean of consciousness that dwarfs the physical world. Infinity pressing against the skin of existence.

The Meridian drones go haywire. Their sensors, designed to read cortical signatures, are overwhelmed by the dimensional bleed. Three crash into the ceiling. Two more spin in circles, their targeting systems corrupted by data they were never built to process.
The moment passes. Reality reasserts itself. But the looks on people's faces have changed. They saw something. They know, now, that they are more than data. More than what can be copied into a stack.
Commander Vex's voice rises over the settling chaos: "FALL BACK! ORGANIZED RETREAT! GET THE WOUNDED OUT FIRST!"

choices.text: Help carry the wounded to safety
Cover the retreat — hold the barricade
Use the chaos to slip away. You've done enough.
[Betrayal] Contact Meridian. Sell out the resistance for your own survival.

climax_042.title: Caught in the Crossfire
text: A drone's taser catches you mid-stride. The electrical discharge locks your muscles and drops you to the platform floor. For a horrifying moment you can't move, can't breathe, can only watch as boots and bodies stampede past your face.

Someone grabs your collar. Drags you. The world tilts and blurs. When your muscles unlock and you can think again, you're behind a barricade, and the face looking down at you belongs to a stranger, a young man with a shaved head and terror in his eyes.
"Stay down," he says. "They're scanning everyone who's still moving. If they flag you, the combat synths—"
He doesn't finish the sentence. A pulse round takes him in the shoulder and he spins away with a cry. He falls hard and doesn't get up.
You're alone behind the barricade. The stranger saved your life and is now bleeding out three meters from you.

choices.text: Drag the stranger behind the barricade and try to help
[Constitution 12] Fight through the taser aftereffects and keep moving
Try to save the stranger, even at risk to yourself

climax_043.title: The Cost of Heroism
text: You throw yourself into the open. The drone spotlights find you instantly. You don't care. The person in front of you is bleeding, dying, and something in you refuses to let another body hit the floor without trying.

You grab them, whoever they are, Kira or the stranger or whoever fate has put in your path, and you pull. Pulse fire stitches the ground around you. The air crackles with energy. Every nerve in your body screams at you to stop, to hide, to save yourself.
You don't stop.

choices.text: [Constitution 13] Endure the fire and reach cover
[Luck 12] Trust to fate

climax_043a.title: The Price
text: The pulse round hits you. Not a taser. Not a dispersal weapon. A lethal round, military-grade, the kind that Meridian swore they wouldn't deploy against civilians.

The impact lifts you off your feet. You feel the heat before the pain, a white-hot line across your torso that tells your brain something very bad has happened before the nerve endings fully process the damage.
You hit the ground. The person you were trying to save is beside you, looking at you with horror. "No," they say. "No, no, no."
The drones move on. You're just another body on the platform. Another casualty in a riot that history will argue about for years. Your vision greys at the edges.

choices.text: [Constitution 14] Refuse to die. Not here. Not like this.
Let go. You saved someone. That's enough.

climax_044.title: The Retreat
text: The organized retreat is barely controlled chaos. Iron Collective soldiers form a rearguard while the wounded are carried out through the maintenance tunnels. Mako moves among the injured, triaging with brutal efficiency, sorting who can walk from who needs carrying from who won't make it.
Commander Vex coordinates from the last barricade, her voice hoarse but unbroken. She sees you and nods once. "You survived. Good. We need everyone who can still stand."

Through the smoke and the settling dust, the Transit Hub looks like a battlefield. Spent taser rounds litter the floor. Barricades are smashed or burning. Three combat synths lie wrecked amid scattered banners and abandoned personal effects. The Meridian line has advanced to the central platform but hasn't pursued the retreat.
They don't need to pursue. They've made their point. The protest is broken. The registration centers will open tomorrow. And everyone who stood here tonight will remember the price of defiance.
Unless the data from the registration center changes everything. Unless the Circuit Saints' corruption of the system holds. Unless the Ashen Circle's revelation took root in enough minds. Unless.

choices.text: Stay with the rearguard until the last civilian is out
Help Mako with the wounded

climax_045.title: The Last Stand at the Barricade
text: The final barricade. Behind you, the last of the wounded disappear into the maintenance tunnels. Ahead, the Meridian line holds its position, combat synths standing like monuments to corporate violence.
Commander Vex stands beside you. Rust. Two orc dockworkers. A synth worker with a cracked chassis. A human teenager who shouldn't be here but refuses to leave. This is what's left.

"They won't advance," Vex says quietly. "They've achieved their objective. The Hub is theirs." She looks at the wreckage of the protest and something in her eyes hardens into diamond. "This isn't over. This is the beginning. Tonight they proved they'll use force. Tomorrow, we prove we won't be stopped by it."
She turns to you. "Get out of here. Regroup. Whatever faction you're with, whatever mission you were running tonight, the real fight starts tomorrow. Tonight was the rehearsal."
She salutes you. It's the first time Commander Vex has ever saluted anyone who isn't Iron Collective.

climax_046.title: Dawn After the Storm
text: You emerge from the tunnels into the grey half-light of a city that has changed. Smoke still rises from the Transit Hub. Emergency drones sweep the streets in widening patterns, their searchlights cutting through the pre-dawn fog. Somewhere distant, a siren wails and dies.

You climb to a rooftop because you need to see it. All of it. The Lower District stretches below you, battered and bruised but breathing. Lights flicker in apartment windows where people who survived the night are checking on each other, counting heads, patching wounds, whispering about what comes next.

The registration centers did not open on schedule. The riot, the data corruption, the dimensional revelation, the sheer stubborn fury of thousands of people refusing to be cataloged, it was enough. Not to win. Not yet. But enough to buy time. Enough to prove that the city is not as obedient as Meridian assumed.
Mako's voice crackles through your comm. "You alive?"
"Alive," you say. The word feels heavier than it used to.

"Good. Because this isn't over. Meridian is regrouping. The factions are fracturing. And whatever that thing was that the Ashen Circle did at the Hub, people are talking about it. People saw something. People are asking questions that Meridian can't answer with drones and registration forms."
She pauses. "Get some rest. Tomorrow the real war starts."
You stand on the rooftop and watch the sun try to break through the cloud layer. It fails, as it always does. But for a moment, just a moment, the light is there. You saw it. And so did the city.

text.text: Your faction will regroup. They will plan. They will remember what happened tonight and use it as fuel for everything that comes next. And you will be there, because you chose a side and held it when the fire came.

You chose no banner. No faction. You survived on your own terms and the city noticed. In the days to come, every faction in the district will remember that you were there, unaligned and unbroken. That makes you dangerous. That makes you valuable. That makes you free.

choices.text: Continue to Act 2

ending_act1_001.title: ENDING: Killed in the Riot
text: The last thing you see is the cold light of a Meridian drone's targeting laser. The last thing you hear is the crack of a pulse weapon at close range. The last thing you feel is the ground rushing up to meet you, hard and final and absolutely indifferent to who you were or what you wanted.

You die in the Transit Hub, surrounded by the wreckage of a protest that was supposed to change everything. Your body is cataloged by a cleanup drone. Your personal effects are bagged and filed. Your face is added to the casualty report that will be classified before morning.

In the Lower District, Mako hears the news and goes quiet for a very long time. Kira cries. Commander Vex adds your name to a list she keeps in a locked drawer, a list of people the system killed who deserved better.
The registration centers open the next day. The city moves on. It always moves on.
END OF STORY — You were killed in the Transit Hub Riot.

ending_act1_002.title: ENDING: Arrested and Stacked
text: They take you alive. That's the worst part. The Meridian security team pins you to the floor of the registration center, a knee on your spine, your face pressed into clinical white tile. The combat synth you failed to stop stands over you, its eyes pulsing with the red light of threat neutralization.

They process you. Not through the regular system. Through the back rooms. The ones the directive doesn't mention. A cortical scanner is pressed against the base of your skull and you feel it reading you, every memory, every thought, every moment of your life copied and compressed into data.
"Subject processed," a technician says. "Full cortical map archived. Body flagged for recycling."

Recycling. They take your body. They keep your mind. You exist now as data in a Meridian server, a consciousness without form, aware and imprisoned in a digital cage that has no walls because it has no dimensions.
Somewhere in the server, you scream. No one hears.
END OF STORY — Your mind was archived. Your body was recycled. You exist as data in a Meridian server. Technically alive. Practically gone.

ending_act1_003.title: ENDING: Fled the City
text: You run. Not toward the protest. Not toward any faction. Not toward anyone. You run from the city, through the tunnels, past the Undercity, through the sealed maintenance hatches that lead to the wasteland beyond the walls.

The wasteland is silence and dust. Behind you, the city's towers glow against the dark sky like the bars of a cage seen from the outside. Somewhere in there, people are fighting and dying for something they believe in. Mako is patching wounds. Commander Vex is holding a barricade. Kira is running from her family.
And you are here. Safe. Alone. Alive in a way that feels indistinguishable from empty.
You walk into the wasteland with nothing but the clothes on your back and the knowledge that you left everyone behind. Some might call it survival. You know the real word for it.
In the Lower District, your room stays empty. After a week, someone else moves in. After a month, no one remembers you were there at all.
END OF STORY — You fled the city. You survived. Was it worth it?

ending_act1_004.title: ENDING: Betrayed Everyone
text: You make the call. Meridian's private line. Director Voss himself picks up on the second ring, as if he'd been waiting.

"I can give you the Iron Collective's leadership. Their safe houses. Their weapons caches. Everything," you say. "In exchange, I want full registration exemption, a new identity, and a mid-level apartment in the corporate sector."
Voss's smile is audible. "Done."

The raids happen at dawn. Iron Collective safe houses are hit simultaneously. Commander Vex is taken alive. Rust goes down fighting. The Foundry burns. Forty-seven people are arrested. Twelve are killed resisting.
Director Voss keeps his word. You get the apartment. The new identity. A life in the corporate sector where the air is clean and the walls are thick enough to muffle the sound of your conscience.

Sometimes, late at night, you hear Mako's voice in the silence. 'I thought I could trust you.' You never hear it from her directly. She disappeared the night of the raids. Ghost Syndicate probably. Or dead. You don't check.
The registration centers open. The city is quiet. You are safe. You are comfortable. You are the worst thing you've ever known.
END OF STORY — You sold everyone to Meridian. You got everything you wanted. You deserve none of it.

ending_act1_005.title: ENDING: Died Saving Mako
text: The pulse round was meant for Mako. You see it coming, the targeting laser painting a dot on her back as she bends over a wounded worker, and your body moves before your mind decides.

You hit her sideways, driving her behind the cargo loader. The round catches you center mass. The force of it is absolute, a hand made of lightning slamming you into the ground with a sound like the world breaking.
Mako screams your name. Her hands, those precise mechanic's hands, press against the wound and come away red. "No," she says, her voice cracking into something raw and broken. "No, not you. Not you."

You want to tell her it's okay. You want to tell her that this was the one clean choice in a dirty city. But the words dissolve somewhere between your brain and your mouth, like signals lost in static.
Mako holds you. Her augmented arm whirs softly against your side, warm with the heat of overworked servos. The last thing you feel is her hand on your face, calloused and gentle and shaking.

In the years that follow, Mako never fully recovers. She keeps your knife on her workbench. She tells people about you when they ask about the scar on her heart. She says you were the best thing the Lower District ever produced.
END OF STORY — You died saving Mako. She never forgot.

ending_act1_006.title: ENDING: The Unnamed Hero
text: You die saving a stranger. You don't even know their name. They're just a person, a face in the crowd, someone who was in the wrong place when the drones opened fire. You put your body between them and the pulse rounds because the alternative was watching another person die.
The stranger lives. They run. They make it out of the Transit Hub and into the tunnels and into a life that continues because you ended yours.

Nobody knows your name. The stranger didn't see your face clearly through the tear gas. The cleanup drones catalog your body as UNIDENTIFIED CASUALTY 47. You are buried in a mass grave at the city's edge with the other unclaimed dead.
But the stranger remembers. They remember the shape of someone stepping in front of them. The sound of a body hitting the ground. The specific weight of surviving because someone else chose not to.

They tell their children about it. An unnamed person who saved their life in the Transit Hub Riot. The children don't fully understand. How could they? But the story survives. And in a city that destroys everything it touches, a story that survives is its own kind of immortality.
END OF STORY — You died saving a stranger. Nobody knows your name. The story survived anyway.

ending_act1_007.title: ENDING: Executed by Your Faction
text: Your faction finds you in the aftermath of the riot. Not as an ally. As a problem.

The evidence is damning. The data siphon in your pocket. The gold comm device from the Neon Court. The contradictory messages on your datapad suggesting you've been playing multiple sides. In the paranoia following the riot, suspicion is a death sentence.

They don't give you a trial. There's no time for trials in a city tearing itself apart. A quiet room. A closed door. Faces you once called allies looking at you with the flat certainty of people who have already made a decision.

"You played both sides," the voice says. It doesn't matter whose voice. It could be any of them. "In the middle of a crisis, when people were dying for something they believed in, you were working angles. Harvesting data. Selling intelligence."
"That's not—" you start.
"It doesn't matter what it is. It matters what it looks like. And it looks like treason."
The door closes. The rest is silence.
END OF STORY — Your own faction executed you for suspected betrayal. Trust is the most expensive commodity in a city that can't afford it.

ending_act1_008.title: ENDING: Stack Overload
text: The black-market stack you accepted from the dealer was always going to do this. You knew it. Somewhere beneath the denial and the hope, you knew that hardware bought in an alley from a stranger with no name would eventually collect its debt.

It happens during the riot. The stress. The adrenaline. The electromagnetic interference from a hundred drones and a thousand pulse weapons. The stack overloads. You feel it as a sound first, a high-pitched whine inside your skull that drowns out the noise of the riot.
Then the pain. Oh god, the pain.
