The stack burns through your neural tissue like acid through paper. Memories dissolve in random order, childhood, last week, your name, the color blue. Your consciousness fragments into shards that don't fit together anymore.

You're on the ground. People step over you. The riot continues. Inside your skull, the black-market stack is consuming everything you are, not to copy it, not to save it, but because the hardware is defective and damaged consciousness is just heat and noise.
The last coherent thought you have is the dealer's smile. And then you don't have thoughts anymore.
END OF STORY — The black-market stack burned your brain. Your consciousness was not preserved. It was destroyed. There is no backup. There is no transfer. There is nothing.

ending_act1_009.title: ENDING: Property of the Neon Court
text: You flee the retrieval team, but you run the wrong way. Instead of the tunnels, you run toward the Neon Strip, toward the glittering lights and the promise of sanctuary. The Neon Court's security intercepts you at the entrance to the Prism Lounge.
Archon Luminara is waiting. Of course she is. She knew you would come. She planned for every contingency. Her gown is the color of a trap closing.
"Poor thing," she says, touching your face with fingers that have never done a day's work. "You've been through so much. Let the Court protect you. All we ask in return is... everything."

The contract is presented. Indentured service to the Neon Court. In exchange for protection, housing, and exemption from registration, you become Court property. Your skills, your body, your loyalty, all of it belongs to Archon Luminara until she decides otherwise.
You sign. What choice do you have? The riot is behind you. Meridian is hunting everyone. The only safety in the city is behind walls owned by people who collect lives like art.

Your new quarters are beautiful. The food is excellent. The cage is gilded and comfortable. And every night, when the Neon Court summons you for whatever task they require, you remember what it felt like to be free.
END OF STORY — You became property of the Neon Court. Comfortable. Protected. Owned.

ending_act1_010.title: ENDING: Consumed by the Void
text: The dimensional anchors activate and the veil thins. For everyone else, it is a moment of revelation, a glimpse of infinity that lasts seconds before reality reasserts itself. For you, it is an invitation.

You are Shadowkin. Or Voidborn. The dimensional frequency that lies beneath reality is not alien to you. It is home. And when the veil opens, the void reaches for you with the tenderness of a mother welcoming a child back from a long journey.

You step forward. Not physically. Something deeper moves, something that has always existed between the cracks of your physical form. The void pulls and you let it. The noise of the riot fades. The pain fades. The city, the towers, the stacks, the corporate tyranny, all of it dissolves like a dream you're waking from.

The void is not empty. It is everything. Every consciousness that ever existed. Every thought that was ever thought. An ocean of being that stretches beyond dimensions, beyond time, beyond the crude limitations of cortical stacks and registration databases.
You dissolve into it. Not dying. Becoming. Expanding beyond the boundaries of a single mind into something vast and ancient and luminous.

In the physical world, your body collapses. The Ashen Circle acolytes find you still breathing but empty, your eyes open and seeing nothing that belongs to this reality. High Priestess Sable kneels beside you and smiles.
"They've gone home," she whispers. "How beautiful."
END OF STORY — You were consumed by the void. Are you dead? Or are you more alive than anyone in the city will ever be? The Ashen Circle has an answer. You no longer need questions.

climax_016_fail.title: Kira Panics
text: Words too tense. Kira freezes in terror.
Your calm shattered against your own fear.

choices.text: Pick Kira up.
Find someone to watch her.

climax_022_fail.title: Schematics Indecipherable
text: Military encryption. Tools barely scratch surface.
Turrets remain a mystery. Time wasted.

choices.text: Go in blind.
Ask if anyone else can read these.

climax_031_fail.title: Architecture Beyond Comprehension
text: Contradictory readings. Passages exist and don't.
No closer to understanding.

choices.text: Navigate by instinct.
Proceed cautiously.

climax_034_fail.title: Luminara Sees Through You
text: She catches the gaps. 'You're holding back.'
Negotiation slips from your grasp.

choices.text: Come clean.
Double down.
